signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk + CESI
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.93 testing the 160.00 psychological barrier as +300bp Fed-BoJ carry dominates despite BoJ hike bias. JPY COT LF net short -86,249 (crowded carry) vs JPY CESI net +11 (data outperforming). While retail is 77% short, the 25d RR at -1.10 signals institutional demand for JPY calls (downside protection) as intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160.00. US labor strength and oil-driven USD bid keep the pair supported until VIX > 25.

signals.title: AUD/CHF — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/CHF at 0.5649 reflects a 0.832% G8 performance spread between the weekly leader (AUD) and laggard (CHF). The divergence is fundamentally anchored by a +435bp carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs SNB 0.0%) and a hawkish RBA stance vs SNB cut bias. However, AUD CESI net -5 (data misses) suggests the outperformance is carry-driven rather than macro-led. Watch Australia GDP on June 3 for potential trend exhaustion.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7181 faces a multi-layer exhaustion signal. COT LF net long +58,041 (76.1% long) represents crowded institutional positioning, while retail is 76% short—a clear institutional vs retail divergence. Options market pricing reflects high anxiety with an IV of 14.9% vs HV30 of 8.0% (VRP +6.9%), the highest in the G10 space. Crowded longs and extreme vol premium suggest limited upside without a significant Australia GDP beat.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Vol Premium + Real Rate Headwinds
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1631 remains structurally capped by a +175bp nominal carry deficit and a restrictive US real rate (+1.22%) vs accommodative EUR real rate (-0.27%). Options market shows elevated hedging demand with a +3.1% VRP (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.0%), though the 25d RR at -0.24 remains near neutral. Oil-driven USD strength and US labor data outperformance (CESI +21) maintain downward pressure toward 1.1600.
